Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Sever Dillingham From The Trial Date
Off Calendar Jun-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Linda I. Yen of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Dillingham Construction, N.A., Inc.
Richard Dumont of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for opposing parties Lamons Gasket Company; and intervenors St. Paul Fire and Marine Insurance Company, et al. on behalf of their suspended insured, defendant Scott Co. of California; and for Intervenor Fireman's Fund Insurance Company on behalf of their suspended insured defendant Association Insulation of California
Erin McGahey of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant / opposing party Timec Company, Inc.
Abigail P. Adams of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING (415-527-2803), appearing for defendant / opposing party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc. (Joinder in Timec Company, Inc.'s opposition)
Michael J. Beler of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant General Electric Company
Michael D. Levinson of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Betty O'Leary as wrongful death heir, and as successor-in-interest to Michael O'Leary, deceased

Ruling:
DEFENDANT DILLINGHAM CONSTRUCTION, N.A., INC.'S MOTION TO SEVER DILLINGHAM FROM THE TRIAL DATE

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.'s motion to sever Dillingham from the trial date is denied. The Court ordered continued the June 13, 2016 trial date to September 26, 2016 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day to hear summary motions is Sept. 22, 2016. Parties stipulate to extend the 5-year statute of limitation from June 20, 2016 to October 3, 2016. A signed stipulation and proposed order will be submitted to the Court for signature. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Betty O'Leary, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating it owed no duty to Mr. O'Leary pursuant to Campbell v. Ford Motor Co. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15, Davis v. Foster Wheeler (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 731 or Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiff's claims against it. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Emily D. Bergstrom of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Betty O'Leary, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT DILLINGHAM CONSTRUCTION, N.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. O'Leary was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
